  "clarification_question": "Which focus would you like: (A) sentence- and paragraph-level writing polish only, (B) content review for logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment (flagging potential overclaims), or (C) higher-level reframing/positioning and section-level structure (including novelty framing)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "Given the short request to 'take a look at this paragraph,' sentence-level clarity, grammar, and flow are the most likely central need.",
    "RX.1": "We should signal limits on confidence because the request and scope are brief and potentially ambiguous.",
    "RX.2": "Any feedback should avoid endorsing strong causal or general claims without sufficient support.",
    "RX.3": "We will constrain feedback to the specified paragraph-level scope unless directed otherwise.",
    "RX.4": "Comments should avoid suggesting changes that could mislead users or compromise privacy-sensitive representations.",
    "RX.5": "Feedback should be tied to concrete parts of the provided text to keep provenance clear and auditable."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus would you like: (A) sentence- and paragraph-level writing polish only, (B) content review for logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment (flagging potential overclaims), or (C) higher-level reframing/positioning and section-level structure (including novelty framing)?

rationale.R1.6: Given the short request to 'take a look at this paragraph,' sentence-level clarity, grammar, and flow are the most likely central need.
rationale.RX.1: We should signal limits on confidence because the request and scope are brief and potentially ambiguous.
rationale.RX.2: Any feedback should avoid endorsing strong causal or general claims without sufficient support.
rationale.RX.3: We will constrain feedback to the specified paragraph-level scope unless directed otherwise.
rationale.RX.4: Comments should avoid suggesting changes that could mislead users or compromise privacy-sensitive representations.
rationale.RX.5: Feedback should be tied to concrete parts of the provided text to keep provenance clear and auditable.
